variants.title: Sharks sense tiny electric fields
variants.source_license: Derived analytics brief; new media required before render
variants.description: Watch the electric sense behind this animal behavior: Sharks sense tiny electric fields
variants.yt_description: Sharks sense tiny electric fields. Watch the electric sense first, then replay the payoff.
variants.hook: Sharks can feel electricity in the water.
variants.script: Sharks can feel electricity in the water. Watch the head before the turn, because sharks have sensory pores that detect tiny electric fields from other animals. That helps them hunt when sight is limited. The ocean is full of signals we cannot feel. Would you swim differently?

variants.title: Horses point their ears toward what matters
variants.source_license: Derived analytics brief; new media required before render
variants.description: Watch the ear focus behind this animal behavior: Horses point their ears toward what matters
variants.seo_title: Horses point their ears toward what matters
variants.yt_description: Horses point their ears toward what matters. Watch the ear focus first, then replay the payoff.
variants.hook: Horses show attention with their ears.
variants.script: Horses show attention with their ears. Watch the ears before the body moves, because each ear can turn toward a sound or animal the horse is tracking. That small shift shows where attention is going before the next step. The ears are a radar dish, not decoration. Would you spot it?

variants.hook: Wolves leave scent notes for the pack.
variants.script: Wolves leave scent notes for the pack. Watch where the animal pauses, because wolves use scent marking to share territory, identity, and timing. A single spot can tell the pack who passed and how recently. The forest has messages we cannot read. Which pack signal next?
